title: hypericum tetrapterum var. corsicum
intro: Hypericum tetrapterum var. corsicum, also known as Corsican St. John's Wort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the St. John's Wort family, characterized by its distinctive four-winged seeds and small, yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal problems. The health benefits of Corsican St. John's Wort include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, promote wound healing, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
